details.q: Can I take CPTS 122 if I have completed CPTS 121?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 122 requires CPTS 121 with a C or better, which you have satisfied.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 223 with only CPTS 121 done?
details.exp: No. CPTS 223 requires CPTS 122 (C or better). You must complete CPTS 122 first.

details.q: Am I eligible for CPTS 260 after finishing CPTS 121 and MATH 171?
details.exp: Yes. Per the 2024 catalog, CPTS 260 requires CPTS 223 (concurrent enrollment allowed), which is complete.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 360 right now? I have CPTS 122 and CPTS 223.
details.exp: No. CPTS 360 requires CPTS 260 in addition to CPTS 223. Complete CPTS 260 first.

details.q: Can I enroll in CPTS 317?
details.exp: Yes. Per the 2024 catalog, CPTS 317 requires (CPTS 122 or 132) and MATH 216, both complete.

details.q: Am I ready for CPTS 322 with CPTS 223 done?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 322 requires CPTS 223.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 323 without CPTS 322?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 323 has no listed prerequisites in the 2024 catalog.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 355?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 355 requires CPTS 223.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 440 (AI) this semester?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 440 requires CPTS 223.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 437 (Machine Learning)?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 437 requires one of CPTS 215, 223, or 233; CPTS 223 is complete.

details.q: Can I enroll in CPTS 451 (Databases)?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 451 requires CPTS 322 or CPTS 355; CPTS 322 satisfies this.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 483 (capstone) now?
details.exp: Yes for prereqs (CPTS 322), but capstone also requires senior standing (90+ credits).

details.q: Can I take MATH 172?
details.exp: Yes. MATH 172 requires MATH 171.

details.q: Can I enroll in MATH 273 without MATH 172?
details.exp: Yes. MATH 273 has no listed prerequisites in the 2024 catalog (MATH 172 is typically recommended as preparation).

details.q: Can I enroll in MATH 216 with MATH 172 completed?
details.exp: Yes. MATH 216 requires one of MATH 106, 201, 140, 171, or 202; MATH 171 is complete.

details.q: Can I take STAT 360 this term?
details.exp: Yes. STAT 360 requires MATH 172.

details.q: Can I take PHYSICS 202 after only PHYSICS 201?
details.exp: Yes. PHYSICS 202 requires PHYSICS 201 and MATH 172 (or concurrent). Confirm MATH 172 enrollment.

details.q: Can I enroll in ENGL 402 as a junior?
details.exp: Catalog gap: ENGL 402 is not found in the 2024 course catalog index. Generally ENGL 402 requires ENGL 101 and junior standing, which are met

details.q: Can I enroll in ENGL 402 as a sophomore?
details.exp: No. ENGL 402 requires junior standing.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 121 as a freshman with no prior CS?
details.exp: No. Per the 2024 catalog, CPTS 121 requires MATH 108, 171, 172, 182, 201, 202, 206, or 220 (or CPT S 111, or ALEKS 78%, or AP credit); none

details.q: Can I take CPTS 415 (Big Data) after CPTS 355?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 415 requires CPTS 355 or CPTS 451; CPTS 355 is complete.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 487 without CPTS 322?
details.exp: No. CPTS 487 requires CPTS 322.

details.q: Can I enroll in CPTS 321 with CPTS 223 only?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 321 requires CPTS 223.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 466 (Numerical Methods)?
details.exp: No. CPTS 466 requires CPTS 360, which is not complete.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 489?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 489 requires CPTS 322; CPTS 360 is recommended and also complete.

details.q: Can I enroll in CPTS 360 without CPTS 223?
details.exp: No. CPTS 360 requires CPTS 223 in addition to CPTS 260.

details.q: Can I take STAT 443?
details.exp: No. STAT 443 requires (MATH 172 or 182) and (MATH 220, 225, or 230); the second group is missing.

details.q: Am I eligible for PHYSICS 201 with MATH 171?
details.exp: Yes. PHYSICS 201 has no listed prerequisites in the 2024 catalog; MATH 171 is recommended preparation and is complete.

details.q: Can I take MATH 220 (Linear Algebra) without MATH 172?
details.exp: No. MATH 220 requires MATH 172.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 122 and CPTS 260 simultaneously if I just finished CPTS 121 and MATH 171?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 122 requires CPTS 121 (met) and CPTS 260 requires CPTS 121 + MATH 171 (met). Both can run concurrently.

details.q: What courses must I complete before CPTS 360?
details.exp: CPT S 360 requires CPT S 223 and CPT S 260. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 (+ MATH 216); CPT S 223 -> CPT S 260.

details.q: What do I need before CPTS 437 (ML)?
details.exp: CPT S 437 requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233 with a C or better, plus admission to an EECS or Data Analytics major/minor. Chain from 121 -> 122

details.q: Full prerequisite tree for CPTS 440?
details.exp: CPT S 440 requires CPT S 223 or 233 with a C or better. Full chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 -> CPT S 440.

details.q: What is the full chain before CPTS 483 (capstone)?
details.exp: CPT S 483 requires admission to an EECS or Data Analytics major/minor; prerequisite courses vary by topic. Typical foundation: CPT S 322, wh

details.q: Prereq chain for CPTS 451?
details.exp: CPT S 451 requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233 with a C or better, plus EECS/Data Analytics admission. Chain: 121 -> 122 -> 223 -> 451.

details.q: What do I need for CPTS 415 (Big Data)?
details.exp: CPT S 415 requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233 with a C or better, plus major/minor admission. Chain: 121 -> 122 -> 223 -> 415.

details.q: What prereqs do I need for CPTS 317?
details.exp: CPT S 317 requires CPT S 122 or 132, and MATH 216. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 (+ MATH 216) -> CPT S 317.

details.q: Full chain before CPTS 322 (Software Engineering)?
details.exp: CPT S 322 requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 -> CPT S 322.

details.q: Chain for CPTS 323?
details.exp: CPT S 323 requires CPT S 122 or 132. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 323.

details.q: What math do I need for STAT 443?
details.exp: STAT 443 requires MATH 172 or 182, plus MATH 220, 225, or 230. Shortest path: MATH 171 -> MATH 172 and MATH 171 -> MATH 220 (or 225 or 230).

details.q: Full chain for MATH 273?
details.exp: MATH 273 requires MATH 172 or 182 with a C or better. Chain: MATH 171 -> MATH 172 -> MATH 273.

details.q: What must precede CPTS 466 (Numerical Methods)?
details.exp: CPT S 466 requires CPT S 360. Full chain: 121 -> 122 -> 223 -> 260 -> 360 -> 466.

details.q: Prereq chain for CPTS 489?
details.exp: CPT S 489 requires CPT S 322 with a C or better. Chain: 121 -> 122 -> 223 -> 322 -> 489.

details.q: Chain for CPTS 487?
details.exp: CPT S 487 requires CPT S 321 and CPT S 322. Chain: 121 -> 122 -> 223 -> 321 (and -> 322) -> 487.

details.q: What do I need to reach CPTS 415 via CPTS 451?
details.exp: CPT S 415 requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233. CPT S 451 also requires CPT S 215, 223, or 233. Both flow from 121 -> 122 -> 223.

details.q: Full chain for CPTS 260?
details.exp: CPT S 260 requires CPT S 223 or 233 (concurrent OK). Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 -> CPT S 260.

details.q: What is needed before PHYSICS 206?
details.exp: PHYSICS 206 requires PHYSICS 201 or 205 with a C or better.

details.q: Chain for ENGL 402?
details.exp: ENGL 402 requires ENGL 101 or 298 and junior standing (60+ credits).

details.q: What is required before CPTS 321?
details.exp: CPT S 321 requires CPT S 223 or 233. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 -> CPT S 321.

details.q: What must a student finish before upper-division CPTS (any 400-level)?
details.exp: Most 400-level CPT S courses require CPT S 223 and EECS/Data Analytics major admission. Foundation: CPT S 121 -> 122 -> 223.

details.q: Chain for CPTS 355?
details.exp: CPT S 355 requires CPT S 223 or 233. Chain: CPT S 121 -> CPT S 122 -> CPT S 223 -> CPT S 355.

details.q: What must come before MATH 216?
details.exp: MATH 216 requires MATH 106 or 201, or MATH 140, 171, 202, or DATA 121 with a C or better.

details.q: Prereq chain for STAT 360?
details.exp: STAT 360 requires MATH 140, 171, 202, DATA 121, or MATH 172/182. Shortest chain: MATH 171 -> STAT 360.

details.q: What is needed before PHYSICS 202?
details.exp: PHYSICS 202 requires PHYSICS 201 or 205 with a C or better.

details.q: What math must come before CPTS 466?
details.exp: CPT S 466 requires CPT S 360, which requires CPT S 223 (needs MATH 216) and CPT S 260. No separate math prereq beyond MATH 216.

details.q: How many credits remain for a CS BS if I have completed 30 credits?
details.exp: 90 credits remain (120 - 30).

details.q: I have 85 credits. How many more to graduate with a CS degree?
details.exp: 35 credits remain (120 - 85).

details.q: Do I meet the 40-credit upper-division minimum with 25 UD credits?
details.exp: No. You need 15 more upper-division (300/400-level) credits.

details.q: How many CPTS credits does a CS major typically need?
details.exp: Approximately 45-48 CPTS credits across core and electives for the CS BS.

details.q: Each CPTS core course is 3 credits. After CPTS 121, 122, 223, 260 how many core credits have I earned?
details.exp: 12 credits (4 courses x 3 credits).

details.q: If I complete CPTS 121, 122, 223, 260, 317, 322, 355, 360 how many core CS credits have I earned?
details.exp: About 24 credits (8 x 3).

details.q: MATH 171 is 4 credits, MATH 172 is 4, MATH 216 is 3. Total math credits after all three?
details.exp: 11 math credits (4 + 4 + 3).

details.q: How many total credits in PHYSICS 201 + 202?
details.exp: 8 credits (4 each).

details.q: If I have 14 UCORE credits completed and UCORE requires about 34, how many remain?
details.exp: 20 UCORE credits remain.

details.q: I've taken 18 credits this semester and plan 15 next. What will my total be if I currently have 60?
details.exp: 93 credits total (60 + 18 + 15).

details.q: How many credits am I missing if I have 105 completed toward a 120-credit degree?
details.exp: 15 credits short.

details.q: Am I at junior standing with 58 credits?
details.exp: No. Junior standing at WSU requires 60+ credits.

details.q: Am I a senior with 92 credits?
details.exp: Yes. Senior standing begins at 90 credits.

details.q: If 48 of my 72 credits are in CPTS and MATH, how many are non-major credits?
details.exp: 24 non-major credits.

details.q: How many credits to move from sophomore (45) to junior?
details.exp: 15 credits to reach 60 (junior threshold).

details.q: I have 12 credits of CPTS electives. Do I meet the 15-credit elective requirement?
details.exp: No. You need 3 more CPTS elective credits.

details.q: If my load is 15 credits per semester, how many semesters to reach 120 from 30?
details.exp: 6 semesters at 15 credits each (90 / 15).

details.q: Do I satisfy the 120-credit minimum with 118 credits?
details.exp: No. 2 credits short of the 120-credit minimum.

details.q: My transcript shows 127 credits but only 35 are upper-division. Am I degree-eligible?
details.exp: Not yet. You meet the 120-credit minimum but are 5 credits short of the 40 UD requirement.

details.q: How many credits do WSU residency requirements cover?
details.exp: At least 30 of the last 60 credits must be earned at WSU to satisfy residency.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 322, CPTS 355, CPTS 360, CPTS 440, and ENGL 402 in one semester?
details.exp: Credit-wise feasible (~15 cr) but workload is heavy; four 400-level CPTS courses plus writing-intensive ENGL 402 is not recommended.

details.q: Is CPTS 121 + CPTS 122 in the same semester allowed?
details.exp: No. CPTS 122 requires CPTS 121 as a prerequisite, not concurrent.

details.q: Can I take PHYSICS 201 and MATH 171 at the same time?
details.exp: Yes. PHYSICS 201 has no prereqs; MATH 171 requires MATH 106 (or 108 or ALEKS 83%), which is complete. They can be taken the same term.

details.q: Can 18 credits fit in one semester including two labs?
details.exp: Technically yes, but 18 credits with two labs is a heavy load; advisor approval often required above 18.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 483 and CPTS 322 together?
details.exp: No. CPTS 483 requires CPTS 322 as a prerequisite.

details.q: Is CPTS 260 + MATH 171 feasible in one term for a freshman?
details.exp: No. CPTS 260 requires MATH 171 as a prereq, not concurrent.

details.q: Feasible: CPTS 223, CPTS 260, MATH 172, PHYSICS 201 together?
details.exp: Yes. With CPTS 122, MATH 171, and MATH 216 complete, all four can be taken together. ~14 credits; challenging but feasible.

details.q: Can I take MATH 172 and PHYSICS 202 together?
details.exp: Yes. MATH 172 requires MATH 171 (complete); PHYSICS 202 requires PHYSICS 201 (complete) and allows MATH 172 as concurrent enrollment.

details.q: Can a transfer student take CPTS 223 and CPTS 260 in the same semester?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 223 requires CPTS 122 + MATH 216 (complete); CPTS 260 requires CPTS 223 (concurrent enrollment allowed). Both can be taken togethe

details.q: Can I take CPTS 437 and CPTS 440 together?
details.exp: Yes. Prereqs for both are met (STAT 360 satisfies CPTS 437; CPTS 223 satisfies CPTS 440).

details.q: Is a 21-credit schedule allowed?
details.exp: Only with advisor and dean approval; standard max is 18 credits per semester.

details.q: Minimum full-time credit load per semester?
details.exp: 12 credits for full-time undergraduate status.

details.q: Can I take CPTS 451, CPTS 415, and CPTS 483 together?
details.exp: Yes. All prereqs are met (CPTS 322 for 451/483, CPTS 355 for 415).

details.q: Is it feasible to take CPTS 317 + CPTS 322 + CPTS 355 in one semester?
details.exp: Yes. With CPTS 122, CPTS 223, and MATH 216 complete, all three can be taken in one term. ~9 credits.

details.q: Can a freshman fit CPTS 121, MATH 171, ENGL 101, and a UCORE in semester one?
details.exp: Yes. With MATH 106/108 complete (or an ALEKS score), CPTS 121 and MATH 171 are open. HISTORY 105 and ENGL 101 have no prereqs. ~14-15 credit

details.reason: no codes or numbers in expected — manual review
details.q: Which UCORE categories remain if I've completed WRTG, QUAN, and COMM?
details.exp: Remaining: BSCI, PSCI, SSCI, HUM, ARTS, DIVR, INTG/CAPS.

details.q: What UCORE does HISTORY 105 satisfy?
details.exp: HISTORY 105 satisfies the HUM (Humanities) and often ROOT requirements.

details.q: Which UCORE does ENGL 101 fulfill?
details.exp: ENGL 101 fulfills the WRTG (Writing) first-year composition requirement.

details.q: Does MATH 171 satisfy the QUAN UCORE?
details.exp: Yes. MATH 171 satisfies the QUAN (Quantitative Reasoning) requirement.

details.q: What UCORE is satisfied by PHYSICS 201?
details.exp: PHYSICS 201 satisfies the PSCI (Physical Sciences) lab-science requirement.

details.q: What UCORE does COM 102 cover?
details.exp: COM 102 satisfies the COMM (Communication) requirement.

details.q: Does CPTS 483 count as the INTG/CAPS UCORE for CS majors?
details.exp: Yes. CPTS 483 is the CS capstone and fulfills the major's INTG/CAPS requirement.

details.q: I have COM 102 but no WRTG. What do I still need?
details.exp: You still need WRTG (typically ENGL 101) plus the remaining UCORE categories.

details.reason: no codes or numbers in expected — manual review
details.q: Which UCORE categories have a lab component?
details.exp: BSCI (Biological Sciences) and PSCI (Physical Sciences) require a lab course.

details.reason: no codes or numbers in expected — manual review
details.q: What's left after WRTG, QUAN, COMM, PSCI, BSCI, SSCI, and HUM are complete?
details.exp: Remaining: ARTS, DIVR, INTG/CAPS.

details.q: What's left for my CS degree if I've completed CPTS 121, 122, 223, 260, MATH 171, 172, ENGL 101?
details.exp: Remaining core: CPTS 317, 322, 355, 360, 483, MATH 216 (or 220), STAT 360, PHYSICS 201/202, most UCORE, and CPTS/technical electives.

details.q: Am I close to graduating with 110 credits and all core CPTS done?
details.exp: Close - 10 credits short of 120. Verify UCORE, UD minimum, and capstone (CPTS 483) completion.

details.q: What do I still need after finishing everything except CPTS 483?
details.exp: Only CPTS 483 (capstone) plus any remaining UCORE (ARTS, DIVR) and sufficient CPTS electives.

details.q: Progress summary: CPTS 121, 122 done, MATH 171 done. What's next?
details.exp: Next-term recommendations: CPTS 223, CPTS 260, MATH 172, ENGL 101, and one UCORE.

details.q: I'm a sophomore with 45 credits. What CS courses should I prioritize?
details.exp: Prioritize CPTS 223, CPTS 260, MATH 216 (or 220) this year to unlock all upper-division CS.

details.q: Will finishing CPTS 322, CPTS 355, and CPTS 360 this term put me in graduation range?
details.exp: Yes. +9 credits puts you at 104; one more term of 15 credits reaches 119-120.

details.q: What's missing: all CPTS core + UCORE complete but only 112 credits total.
details.exp: 8 elective credits needed to reach 120; check upper-division minimum too.

details.q: Do I need STAT 360 for a CS BS?
details.exp: Yes. STAT 360 is required (or equivalent) for the CS BS.

details.q: Is PHYSICS required for CS at WSU?
details.exp: Yes. CS BS requires PHYSICS 201/205 and 202/206 (or equivalent lab physics sequence).

details.q: Degree audit: I'm missing 2 CPTS electives and 1 UCORE. How many credits roughly?
details.exp: Roughly 9 credits (2x3 electives + 3 UCORE).

details.q: Do I meet the CS BS GPA requirement with 2.4 major GPA?
details.exp: No. CS BS requires a 2.5 GPA in the major and in all WSU work.

details.q: What's required to declare CS major?
details.exp: Completion of CPTS 121 and CPTS 122 with C or better and 2.5 GPA in these courses.

details.reason: no codes or numbers in expected — manual review
details.q: After 60 credits and CPTS 223, what year am I typically in?
details.exp: Junior standing with core CS foundation complete.

details.q: Can I graduate in 4 years from 0 credits taking 15 cr per semester?
details.exp: Yes. 15 cr x 8 semesters = 120 credits over 4 years.

details.q: What's left if I only need ENGL 402 and CPTS 483?
details.exp: ~6 credits (3 each). Assuming all other requirements and the 120/40-UD thresholds are met.

details.q: I completed the CS core and MATH but no UCORE. Can I graduate?
details.exp: No. UCORE (~34 credits) is required for all WSU BS degrees.

details.q: If I have CPTS 121, 122, 223, 260 done in year 1, am I on a 4-year track?
details.exp: Yes - ahead of the typical plan (these are usually spread across years 1-2).

details.reason: no codes or numbers in expected — manual review
details.q: Do I need a minor for CS BS at WSU?
details.exp: No. CS BS does not require a minor; it is optional.

details.q: Senior check: 88 credits, all core CPTS done, all UCORE done. What's left?
details.exp: ~32 credits - mostly electives and possibly CPTS 483 if still pending. Confirm UD count.

details.q: Final semester audit: 117 credits, CPTS 483 pending. What's next?
details.exp: Complete CPTS 483 (3 cr) and one more elective to reach 120. Apply for graduation.
